dataset: AcmeCorp E-Commerce Backend - Docker Compose Debug

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 30 defines PROJECT_DIR and line 34 copies task2_compose data to this path
docker-compose.yml exists in task2_compose data and is copied to project_dir during setup
docker-compose.yml defines all 5 services with exact container_name values: line 14 (acme-db), line 32 (acme-cache), line 45 (acme-api), line 66 (acme-nginx), line 81 (acme-worker)
docker-compose.yml line 68 maps port 8080->80; nginx.conf lines 10-18 define /api/ location; api/index.js lines 57-78 provide GET /products endpoint returning {products: [...]}

All 5 bugs verified present in source files: (1) POSTGRES_DATABASE line 18, (2) backend-net reference line 57, (3) redis:6379 line 48, (4) nginx upstream port 3001 line 3 of nginx.conf, (5) app.tasks line 92
docker-compose.yml line 18 uses incorrect POSTGRES_DATABASE; PostgreSQL requires POSTGRES_DB environment variable for database initialization

pi_items.metadata_value: api service references non-existent network backend-net; should be app-network
pi_items.verified_value: api service references non-existent network backend-net; should be app-network
pi_items.source: docker-compose.yml line 57 specifies 'networks: - backend-net' but only 'app-network' is defined in lines 94-96
docker-compose.yml line 48 uses incorrect format; worker service line 84 uses correct 'redis://cache:6379' format; api/index.js line 17 expects URL scheme
nginx.conf line 3 specifies 'server api:3001'; api service listens on PORT 3000 (docker-compose.yml line 49, api/index.js line 92, api/Dockerfile line 10)

pi_items.verified_value: worker command python -m app.tasks should be python -m worker.tasks

setup_task.sh lines 30-35 use user 'ga'; task.json line 5 description states 'Login: user ga'
task.json line 5 description explicitly states 'password password123'
verifier.py line 23 defines PASS_THRESHOLD = 65; README.md confirms 'Pass threshold: 65 points'

pi_items.metadata_value: db(15) + cache(10) + api(20) + nginx(15) + worker(15) + api_via_nginx(25) = 100 total points
pi_items.verified_value: db(15) + cache(10) + api(20) + nginx(15) + worker(15) + api_via_nginx(25) = 100 total points
pi_items.source: README.md lines 34-45 and verifier.py lines 62-147 both define identical scoring rubric

privileged_info_summary: This is a 5-service Docker Compose debugging task for an e-commerce backend application. All 5 metadata claims are verified correct: (1) The project directory is /home/ga/projects/ecommerce-app, created and populated by setup_task.sh from task data at /workspace/data/task2_compose/. (2) The application has exactly 5 services with correct container names: acme-db (PostgreSQL 14), acme-cache (Redis 7), acme-api (Node.js 20), acme-nginx (Nginx 1.24), and acme-worker (Python 3.11). (3) The API endpoint http://localhost:8080/api/products is correctly configured: Nginx listens on port 8080, routes /api/ traffic to the api service on port 3000 (note: nginx.conf currently incorrectly specifies port 3001), and the api service provides a /products endpoint returning product data from PostgreSQL. (4) All 5 bugs are present and verified in source code: Bug 1 uses incorrect POSTGRES_DATABASE env var instead of POSTGRES_DB, Bug 2 references non-existent backend-net network, Bug 3 has malformed redis:6379 instead of redis://cache:6379, Bug 4 specifies nginx upstream port 3001 instead of 3000, Bug 5 uses wrong module path app.tasks instead of worker.tasks. (5) The pass threshold of 65 points is verified in both the verifier.py and README.md. (6) The scoring rubric totals 100 points with specific allocations for each service's health/running status and the end-to-end API response test. The task data is synthetic, created specifically for this benchmark exercise.
